summary:
Galantamine induces gastrointestinal side effects like vomiting, cramps, and watery diarrhea due to increased acetylcholine-mediated stimulation of muscarinic receptors. Atropine is the most appropriate treatment because it directly reverses this muscarinic overactivation by acting as a competitive antagonist at peripheral muscarinic receptors.  

\boxed{C}

\boxed{C}. Answer choice: C
